rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Notice for the amendment is defective and appears must be re-done. 2) Examiner requested legal authority for why interest was paid on the gift to Marie Amoudjaian when Article 11 of the Will says, "No interest shall be paid on any gift or legacy under this Will or any Codicil to it." Petitioner alleges a former San Francisco Examiner provided notes on 05/08/2018 indicating that Marie Amoudjaian was entitled to interest. Please file a copy of the notes. 3) Please file a file stamped copy of the first page of the complaint attached to the 7/19/2021 Sur-Reply to Objection to Petition For Final Distribution. 4) The Amended Petition appears to request distribution that is different from Decedent's Will and does not include all the directions indicated in the Will. Article Fourth B. of the Will states "I give two (2) shares to the Los Angeles Chapter of the Central Committee of the Armenian Revolutionary Federation of the Western United States with directions that said bequest to it shall be invested in US government bonds or bonds suitable for investment under the prudent man rule and the income therefrom shall be used for the preparation and education of field workers in the pursuit of the furtherance of the Armenian Cause (HAI TAD)." The Amended Petition requests distribution to the "The Armenian Revolutionary Federation 20%): the sum of $147,446.23 with said amount to be prudently invested, with the income used for the preparation and education of field workers in the pursuit and furtherance of the Armenian Cause." 5) Court to determine status on filing a second account.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GERTHA R CURTIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgement Of Conservator To Create And Execute A Revocable Trust And Pour-Over Will; To Fund Trust; And For Apporval Of Gifting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/2021. 1) The judicial officer requested a track-changes copy of the amended proposed trust instrument. Examiner has not received the track-changes copy that was requested by the judicial officer. 2) Prior examiner notes stated all investment and bank accounts are to be listed on schedule a. Schedule A in the proposed order does not match schedule a in the second amendment. The proposed order must match what is requested in the second amendment. 3) Prior examiner notes stated the order should state only that Paula Bibbs is authorized (not directed) to execute the trust and will. The language in the prayer of the second amendment still says authorized and directed. The proposed order must match the language in the second amendment. 4) Prior examiner notes stated the statements that her appointments are approved should be stricken. Second Amendment page 5 lines 15 and 19 still says the court approve the appointments. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/4/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF RICHARD PATUREL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/3/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Amended LETTERS have not yet been filed, as noted by the Court Investigator. It now appears that the conservator is seeking to amend the order appointing him in order to obtain authority, after the fact, to pay himself fees on account. In any event, if the amended LETTERS are not filed prior to 10/22/21, the hearing will be continued again. The original financial statements do not appear to verify balances immediately preceding the date of the conservator's appointment. Most of them are titled in the conservatorship estate, not in the conservateeâs name. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2).] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/4/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if notice should have been given for the amended ("revised") accounting attached to the "Declaration" filed on 7/12/21 and if (1) the trustee's declaration in support of her compensation, resubmitting her handwritten time sheets without totals and showing that she charges $75 per hour, is sufficient, (2) attorney fees are reasonable, and (3) the trustee's distribution of all income to herself is authorized by the testamentary trust and should be approved. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ALLEN ROGERS SIMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/2021 1) On 07/12/2021 the court rejected the $800.00 check. The proof of payment of assessment fee (local court form) must be filed before the most recent accounting will be reviewed. 2) Examiner requested a legal basis and evidence/receipts for Allenda Simpson being reimbursed for half the total payoff the first loan. No evidence/receipts filed. Matter continued again for counsel to file evidence/receipts. If no receipt(s) are available, please file legal basis/cite legal authority for the court granting the request with no receipt(s). 3) Examiner requested a receipt for entry 10/09/2020 payroll deposit for $5,137.48. An invoice was filed not a receipt. Matter continued again for counsel to file a receipt. If no receipt is available please file legal basis/cite legal authority for the court granting the request with no receipt. 4) Examiner requested a receipt for entry 10/30/2020 Payroll for 10/16/20 to 10/31/2020 for $2,916.95. An invoice was filed not a receipt. Matter continued again for counsel to file a receipt. If no receipt is available, please file legal basis/cite legal authority for the court granting the request with no receipt. 5) Examiner requested a receipt for entry 01/06/2020 Rent & Food for Nov & Dev for $7,000.00. No receipts filed. Matter continued again for counsel to file receipt(s). If no receipt(s) are available, please file legal basis/cite legal authority for the court granting the request with no receipt(s). 6) Examiner requested a receipt for entry 1/6/2020 for 50% of expenses for Broderick Property for $3,780.62. No receipt(s) filed. Matter continued again for counsel to file receipt(s). If no receipt(s) are available, please file legal basis/cite legal authority for the court granting the request with no receipt(s). 7) Examiner requested a receipt for entry 4/20/2020 for emergency repair-Broderick for $795.00. An invoice filed no receipt(s) filed. Matter continued again for counsel to file receipt(s). If no receipt(s) are available, please file legal basis/cite legal authority for the court granting the request with no receipt(s). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/4/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LAURENCE WILLIAM TINSLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/12/2021. 1) Please file receipts for all requests for reimbursement to Huber Fox that include service fees. 2) Please explain how the $75.00 services fee to one legal was a benefit to the estate. Please provide legal basis/cite legal authority in support of paying the service fee. 3) Please explain how the $80.00 services fee to file and serve express was a benefit to the estate. Please provide legal basis/cite legal authority in support of paying the service fee. 4) Please explain why the estate should pay for a telephonic appearance $94.00 for court call when the court minutes indicate Jonathan Huber appeared via zoom on the 03/02/2021 hearing which is free. Please provide a legal basis/cite legal authority in support of this request. 5) Please explain how paying $340.73 for "service frees (courtesy copy delive" is a benefit to the estate when these high fees could be avoided by mailing documents to the court. Please explain the legal basis/cite legal authority in support of reimbursement of the request. 6) Please explain why there appears to be a duplicate request for a filing fee for $435.00. There is only 1 filing fee for $435.00 in the court system. Please explain entries "5/2021 File and serve express service free (filing fee $435.00, service fee $80.00 ) for $515.00 and the last line on exhibit C entry [no date] San Francisco County Superior Court Filing Fee - Final for $435.00. 7) Please explain the circumstances regarding the request for reimbursement of hotels and gas. (See LRSF 14.58). Please explain the legal basis/cite legal authority in support of reimbursement of the request. 8) Please explain how the court can determine the entry 10/29 for $350.00 was for a burial plot. Please file a receipt. If no receipt is available, please explain the legal basis/cite legal authority in support of the request. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/27/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY FLORENCE CROWLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Final Distribution Of Estate Assets On Waiver Of Account And Request For Compensation To Attorneys For Ordinary Services
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/3/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The administratorâs compensation is missing from the caption of the petition. [CRC 7.102.] There is no Exhibit A attached to the Waiver of Accounting signed by Sandra Crowley, individually, not as the personal representative of the post-deceased sisterâs estate. A copy of Letters certified within 60 days must be filed. [LRSF 14.36 G.] The filing fee for this petition was $435, not $450. Why is the decedent's income tax return required for 2020 when she died in 2018? A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security (not just "stocks" or "IRA accounts"). [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Paragraph 13 of the report says a $15,000 reserve will be maintained (presumably, meaning "requested"), but paragraph 34.a. of the report and paragraph 7 of the prayer mentions $20,000. If tangible personal property has already been distributed, receipts must be filed. It is insufficient to reference a related case and papers filed therein; a petition in this estate must stand on its own. (The administrator was appointed on 1/8/20, not 1/9/20. Letters of Administration, not Testamentary, were issued to her. There are no devisees of this intestate estate, only heirs. Rev. & Tax. C. 19513 was repealed. California has no estate tax.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution". [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. (Also, an order IS a proposed order until it is signed; there is no need to put "[Proposed]" in the caption.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/22/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JEREMIAS ZEULI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/2/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. Notice was not given to the conservatee. (The conservator does not need to give notice to herself of her own petition.) No Corrected I&A has been filed, as alleged in paragraph 4 of the petition. Original bank statements or original letters signed by an officer of the bank having the authority to sign have not been filed, as alleged in paragraph 8 of the petition. Nor have computer-generated printouts been filed with the required declaration. [LRSF 14.35 D. and CRC 7.575(b).] The accounting is incomplete: there is no schedule of property on hand attached. There are reverse mortgage receipts starting in December 2020. Under what authority was a reverse mortgage obtained? A liabilities schedule must be part of the accounting. The detailed schedules supporting the summary of account must show the nature or purpose of each receipt or disbursement, the source of the receipt or name of the payee, and the date thereof. [Prob. C. Sect. 1062.] Reimbursements to anyone, especially the conservator, must clearly indicate who the original payee was. Attorney fees are requested in the prayer but are not mentioned in the petition. No time sheets are attached to the petition and no separate fee declaration has been filed. The bond actually appears to be excessiveâ¦ (The conservatee is receiving "education"? See paragraph 6. He is definitely not a minor. See paragraph 9.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/22/21. If issues are not fully addressed in time to deliver the courtesy copies of such responsive pleadings to the assigned Examiner by 10/22/21, an appearance will be required, as the records reflect that this conservator (and/or her attorney) have not been diligent about meeting the statutory requirements or complying with Court orders. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM MICHAEL HENRY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition has been pending for almost a year since the initial hearing in October 2020. At the last hearing on 06/22/2021, counsel for the petitioner was advised that the settlor's heirs must be served in the manner required for service of a summons, absent a personal representative of the settlor's probate estate, as the settlor did not leave any will. There are at least 17 first cousins once removed who would be the heirs, but only 9 of them have been served, according to the proofs of service on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT P. CABAJ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Equitable, Non Pro-Rata Division Of Real Property And Approval Of Distribution Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Probate Code 11950 allows the filing of a petition for partition/allotment when beneficiaries "have not agreed among themselves to a partition, allotment, or other division of the property." Here, the parties have agreed. Thus, it does not appear that a proper request is being made under this code section. 2) The parties "agree" that the court approve and order Petitioner to obtain an encumbrance pursuant to the agreement. No legal authority regarding Petitioner's ability to encumber real property has been discussed. The terms of the anticipated loan have not been provided. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE MARGARET STEINKELLNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/12/2021. Continued a third time for same issues. If no response filed the matter will go on hearing for the sole purpose of taking the matter off calendar for failure to respond. 1) In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)] The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)]. 2) Must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/27/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF REYNOLD DUBBERKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/21/21. Notice must be mailed by a neutral third party, not the petitioner. (See item 1 of the Proof of Service.) Notice of the hearing on 9/21/21 must be given at least 15 days before that hearing by someone who is not an interested party. (The mandatory Notice of Petition to Administer Estate is the only proper form of notice at this point. Please do NOT use Civil forms in Probate.) A courtesy copy of the proof of service must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/10/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIGUEL Q URIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/21/21 to be heard with competing petition. Additionally, the following issues will need to be addressed: No proof of publication has been filed. No proposed order has been submitted or received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 41.60 E.] Notice mailed on 8/24/21 was defective for a hearing on 9/7/21. (And no courtesy copy of the proof of service has been received by the assigned Examiner. It was reviewed online as a one-time courtesy. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.]) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. The unsigned and undated will is not a holographic instrument; it is a statutory will form. Under what authority can the Court admit a statutory will form that is not properly executed, as the form clearly instructs? The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18. D.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/10/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
